summary: A chart bump lowered payments's memory limit to 16Mi (from 128Mi), OOM-killing the container on startup and driving it into CrashLoopBackOff ~95s later.

confidence.rationale: OOMKilled (confirmed) with exitCode 137 is an unambiguous memory-limit kill; the alert fired 95s after the rollout (inside the correlation window); the only changed field is the memory limit.

timeline.label: payments pods OOMKilled and entered CrashLoopBackOff (6 restarts observed)

resource_change.note: The chart bump lowered payments's memory limit to 16Mi, below the container's ~40Mi startup ballast working set. The kubelet OOM-kills the container before it becomes ready, so it enters CrashLoopBackOff. Restoring the limit to 128Mi is a one-field forward-fix.

evidence.detail: charts/ballast-service values for payments set resources.limits.memory=16Mi; the previous healthy value was 128Mi. This is the only field that changed.
Pod container state: waiting.reason=CrashLoopBackOff, lastState.terminated.reason=OOMKilled, exitCode=137, restarts=6. OOMKilled + exitCode 137 is a memory-limit kill, not a code crash.
BallastServiceCrashLooping is firing; alert activeAt 2026-07-01T12:31:35Z is 95s after the rollout at 2026-07-01T12:30:00Z (within the 600s correlation window).

supporting_telemetry.observation: Value 1 for payments — the kubelet is holding the container in CrashLoopBackOff.
Restart count climbing (6 observed) with backoff.

blast_radius.note: 4 service(s) depend on payments (checkout, ledger, notifications, orders). A full chart rollback churns payments again and briefly disrupts all of them; a targeted memory-limit forward-fix touches only payments.

recommended_action.reasoning: The regression is a single field (memory limit 128Mi -> 16Mi) and the fix is trivial and low-risk. A full rollback of the chart bump would also revert any unrelated changes in it and re-roll payments, disrupting 4 downstream service(s): checkout, ledger, notifications, orders. Prefer a targeted forward-fix that restores the limit only.
recommended_action.remediation: helm upgrade payments charts/ballast-service -n ballast --reuse-values --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi   # or set it back in deploy/services/payments.values.yaml and let ArgoCD sync
